behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module-to-client handoff: the payload can be correct in a local builder but dropped/mutated before dispatch. An in-process fake API client that captures outgoing payload is sufficient. Rejected System because behavior text is about request fields, not real network/OS effects. Rejected Agentic because correctness does not depend on model judgment, only payload composition.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a fake provider client, invoke classify(), and assert the captured request contains exactly the full configured taxonomy label list on every call.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is response-handling across module boundary (provider response -> module output). A fake provider returning an out-of-taxonomy label can reproduce the real failure without network. Rejected Agentic because behavior text fixes the returned label value, so model-quality variability is not under test.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake provider that returns an invalid label (e.g., "medical"), call classify(), and assert returned label is "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is deterministic normalization logic on identical inputs; no module wiring or I/O is required to trigger the failure. Rejected Integration because the behavior text explicitly scopes to the normalization step itself.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function/object twice (or property-test many times) with the same raw response fixture and assert identical normalized label output.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on repeated live AI API outputs and their potential variance. Mocking would hide real model/provider nondeterminism. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification calls, not a multi-stage agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real provider calls for identical input under stable settings, record raw provider response + final label, and assert label drift only occurs when provider response content changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure mode is ordering/handoff: budget enforcement must occur before dispatch path. This is best caught by wiring budget logic with dispatch client and asserting no over-budget request is sent. Rejected System because real network/OS behavior is not required. Rejected Agentic because token-budget enforcement here is local policy logic, not model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Configure low token budget, use a fake provider client, send oversized document, and assert module either truncates/rejects per policy and never dispatches an over-budget payload.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Requires real AI provider behavior/token accounting; a stubbed usage field would always pass and miss real tokenizer/model-side usage differences. Rejected Integration because correctness depends on real provider-reported AI API output, not just local orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification calls for representative documents, capture provider-reported usage metadata, and assert usage <= configured budget with alert/failure on overage.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is retry orchestration (error handling, backoff, retry count, eventual success) across module and provider client boundary. In-process fake provider + controllable clock can reproduce it. Rejected System because real network timing is not required to validate logic; rejected Agentic because no dependence on model decision content.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake provider that returns rate-limit error then success; with fake clock/assertable sleeper, verify module waits/backs off, retries, and returns successful label instead of surfacing immediate error.
